mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor demand and wage growth, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE drops > 0.5pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized expansion across manufacturing and construction sectors, which historically precedes broad-based earnings growth and positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO and HOUST above their 12-month moving averages fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers market repricing of future monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL increases by more than 2.0% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive sequential growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly additions to nonfarm payrolls signal immediate labor market tightening and consumer spending power, prompting algorithmic and discretionary buying that lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS increases by more than 500,000 over a three-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rates and elevated residential fixed investment amplifies household wealth through housing appreciation, generating a consumption-driven equity rally over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and RSAFS above its 24-month average fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks or weakening manufacturing demand, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of quarters where INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% over three months.
